q: The pitch of the note is determined by its frequency.
The greater the frequency, the higher is the pitch.
The loudness of the note depends upon the wavelength that produces it.
The greater the amplitude, the louder the note.
Quality or timbre distinguishes two sounds of different loudness and pitch.
Timbre of a sound depends on its waveform.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the square of the amplitude of the wave.
Loudness is a physical property that does not depend on the observer.
To human ears, the loudness of the sound is subjective.
Normally the greater the intensity, the greater the loudness of the sound is.
Quality of sound varies with the number of overtones or harmonics.
The intensity of sound is the energy transmitted per second per unit area held normally to direction of sound.
